revision.text:
[[ja:石馬谷古墳]][[zh:石馬谷古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ishiumadani Kofun | image = [[File:Ishiumadani Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part to the left, rear circle to the back right) | alternate_name = Koeda-yama No. 5 Kofun {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} | part_of = [[Mukouyama Kofun Group]] (part of Koeda-yama Kofun Group) | location = [[Yonago City]], {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}}, Yodoe Town, Fukuoka | coordinates = {{Coord|35.451958333333|N|133.44266666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|27|7.05|N |26|33.60|E | omitted = JP-31 | type = [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] | area = Length 61.2m
Height 5m (rear circle) | burial = Unknown | finds = Stone horse, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} | satellites = | built = Mid {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} | Interred_individual = | tomb = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site|1=Historic site}} (part of the "Mukouyama Kofun Group") | tangible_notes = Stone horse (National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) | notable = Only example of {{ill|Sekijin sekiba|en|Sekijin sekiba|qid=Q17994947|lt=stone human and horse figures|1=Sekijin sekiba}} on Honshu | map= {{location map|Japan Tottori|coordinates=35|coordinates=27|coordinates=7.05|lon_deg=133|lon_min=26|lon_sec=33.6|map_dot_label= Ishiumadani Kofun|mark=Keyhole.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Tottori Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|7.05|N|133|26|33.6|E}}}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Ishiumadani Kofun''' (石馬谷古墳, Koeda-yama No. 5 Kofun) is an ancient [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in [[Yonago City]], {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}}, Yodoe Town, Fukuoka. It is part of the [[Mukoyama Kofun Cluster|Mukouyama Kofun Group]] and specifically belongs to the Koeda-yama Kofun Group. The tumulus has been Historic site as part of a National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site|1=Historic site}}, and a stone horse excavated from the site is a Historic site National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. The kofun is notable for being the only site on Honshu where {{ill|Sekijin sekiba|en|Sekijin sekiba|qid=Q17994947|lt=stone human and horse figures|1=Sekijin sekiba}} (seijin-seiba) have been found. == Overview == The kofun is located on the slope of Koeda-yama Hills, in the eastern part of the Yodoe Plain in western Tottori Prefecture. It was partially excavated in {{ill|1989|en|1989|qid=Q2425|lt=1989|1=1989}} (Heisei 1). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented southwest. The mound is constructed in two tiers. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Its total length measures 61.2 meters. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The surface of the mound features [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) as well as [[cylindrical haniwa]] (including morning-glory-shaped haniwa) and [[Representational Haniwa|Figurative haniwa]] (human-shaped and shield-shaped haniwa). {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The burial facility remains unknown. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} Other burial goods include a stone horse and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, with the stone horse being especially significant as the only such example found on Honshu. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The kofun is believed to have been built in the mid-{{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} during the late [[Kofun period]]. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The site was Historic site as part of a National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site|1=Historic site}} in {{ill|1999|en|1999|qid=Q2091|lt=1999|1=1999}} (Heisei 11) under the "Mukouyama Kofun Group," and its stone horse was Historic site a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in {{ill|1959|en|1959|qid=Q5302|lt=1959|1=1959}} (Showa 34). == Chronology == * {{ill|1902|en|1902|qid=Q2043|lt=1902|1=1902}} (Meiji 35): [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]] identified the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * During the [[:en:Taisho period|Taisho period]] (1912–1926): Stone fragments were collected near the front part of the tumulus, and the stone horse at Tenjingaki Shrine was confirmed to have originated from this kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} * {{ill|1959|en|1959|qid=Q5302|lt=1959|1=1959}} (Showa 34): The stone horse was Historic site a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. * {{ill|1989|en|1989|qid=Q2425|lt=1989|1=1989}} (Heisei 1): The site underwent trial excavation by the Yodoe Town Board of Education, and a report was published in {{ill|1990|en|1990|qid=Q2064|lt=1990|1=1990}}. * {{ill|1999|en|1999|qid=Q2091|lt=1999|1=1999}} (Heisei 11): The site was Historic site as part of the National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site|1=Historic site}} "Mukouyama Kofun Group." {{National Historic site Database|401|2110|Mukouyama Kofun Group}} == Kofun == The area of the tumulus are as follows: {{PDFlink|[http://yonagobunka.net/system/site/upload/live/12551/atc_1522851322.pdf Yonago City Archaeological Center Newsletter No. 28]}} (Yonago City Archaeological Center, 2018). * Total length: 61.2 meters * Rear circle diameter: 34.5 meters * Rear circle height: 5 meters The tumulus is the second-largest in the Yodoe area, following Mukouyama No. 4 Kofun (64.5 meters). == burial goods == [[File:石馬谷古墳出土 石馬 (複製).JPG|thumb|250x250px|{{center|Stone horse (replica)}}{{small|Exhibited at the Hakuho Hill Museum. The original is a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].}}]] The most notable artifact from Ishiumadani Kofun is the '''stone horse'''. It is a stone representation of a haniwa horse and part of the broader category of "{{ill|Sekijin sekiba|en|Sekijin sekiba|qid=Q17994947|lt=stone human and horse figures|1=Sekijin sekiba}}" (seijin-seiba). The stone, identified as hornblende {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|qid=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}} from Mount Daisen, measures approximately 150 cm in length and 90 cm in height. Intypeational plaque at Tenjingaki Shrine (Tottori Prefecture Board of Education, 2015). Although the front legs are missing, the surface shows detailed carvings of tack such as bridles, reins, and a sa11le. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} The stone horse is believed to have been originally painted red. {{Sfn|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}} While stone human and horse figures are more commonly found in northern Kyushu, such as those from the [[Iwato-yama Kofun]] in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, the Ishiumadani Kofun example is the only one found on Honshu. The stone’s origin indicates local craftsmanship but reflects cultural interactions between the San’in region and northern Kyushu. The stone horse was enshrined as "Stone Horse Daimyojin" during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} and was first documented by Tsuboi Shōgorō in 1902. Further excavations during the Taisho period confirmed its connection to Ishiumadani Kofun. {{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}{{Sfn|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}} The artifact is now stored in the Tenjingaki Shrine repository. == Historic site == === {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} (National) === * Stone Horse (archaeological artifact) - Originally located at Tenjingaki Shrine, Fukuoka, Yodoe Town, typeerly Saihaku District. Designated on December 18, 1959. {{National Historic site Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}}[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] (Tottori Historic site Navigator).[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] (Yonago City Official Website). == References == == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for writing this article:}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/secure/16781/mukouyama.pdf Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yonago City Board of Education)|ref={{Harvid|Mukouyama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}}} * {{Cite book|author=Ōtsuka Hatsushige|year=1989|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns]]|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|year=1992|chapter=Ishiumadani Kofun|title=[[Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan]]: Tottori Prefecture Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Ishiumadani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == * {{National Historic site Database|201|9815|Stone Horse}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/7DCBB322DC32342A4925796F0007FC82?OpenDocument Stone Horse] - Tottori Historic site Navigator * [https://www.city.yonago.lg.jp/16703.htm Stone Horse] - Yonago City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|石馬谷古墳|version=99995156|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:石馬谷古墳]] [[zh:石馬谷古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q65276071}} [[Category:History of Yonago, Tottori]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Tottori prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tottori prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tottori prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
